metadata.subject: Physical and Health Education
metadata.description: Outdoor Education 12 develops advanced outdoor leadership and expertise through complex wilderness expeditions, expedition planning, and sophisticated risk management. Students engage with advanced backcountry skills, lead outdoor trips for peers, and explore environmental science and ecological knowledge. The course emphasizes First Peoples ecological wisdom, understanding of BC ecosystems, and the role of outdoor leadership in environmental conservation and community wellness. Students examine career pathways in outdoor recreation, environmental education, and wilderness therapy, preparing for potential post-secondary study or professional outdoor work.
metadata.general_description: An advanced leadership course developing expedition planning expertise, outdoor mentorship capability, ecological knowledge, and readiness for outdoor recreation careers.

big_ideas.statement: Advanced outdoor leadership involves comprehensive expedition planning, group management, and responsibility for others' safety and development.
big_ideas.elaboration: Grade 12 students take leading roles in planning and executing multi-day wilderness expeditions, managing logistics, group dynamics, and safety systems.

big_ideas.statement: Deep ecological knowledge and First Peoples perspectives on land relationships transform outdoor experience into environmental stewardship.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop sophisticated understanding of ecosystems, interconnected relationships, and Indigenous ecological wisdom that informs their role as land stewards.

big_ideas.statement: Wilderness expeditions are powerful learning experiences that develop resilience, leadership, environmental awareness, and personal transformation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students recognize how extended outdoor time catalyzes personal growth, builds confidence, and deepens connection to landscape and community.

big_ideas.statement: Advanced risk management combines technical knowledge, judgment, and ethical decision-making in complex outdoor contexts.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop sophisticated ability to assess environmental complexity, anticipate potential issues, and make defensible decisions balancing safety with meaningful challenge.

big_ideas.statement: Outdoor recreation and environmental education are viable career pathways contributing meaningfully to community and environmental health.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore careers in outdoor education, environmental science, wilderness therapy, recreation management, and environmental conservation.

categories.name: Expedition Planning and Leadership
categories.raw: Planning and leading complex multi-day wilderness expeditions with responsibility for group safety and development.

competencies.raw: Plan comprehensive expeditions including logistics, safety systems, and learning outcomes; lead expeditions with effective group management; mentor peers in wilderness skills; make sound decisions in changing conditions.
i_can_statements: I can plan a multi-day wilderness expedition comprehensively — including route analysis, supply logistics, shelter and cooking systems, weather preparation, and contingency planning.
I can establish and maintain safety systems for group expeditions — including hazard communication, emergency protocols, and continuous risk monitoring.
I can manage group dynamics, resolve conflicts, and maintain team cohesion and morale across multi-day expeditions.
I can mentor peers in wilderness skills, providing skilled instruction and encouragement while supporting their learning and confidence development.
I can make sound decisions in dynamic outdoor situations, balancing safety with meaningful challenge, consulting team input, and adjusting plans based on changing conditions.

categories.name: Advanced Wilderness Skills and Expertise
categories.raw: Demonstrating expert-level competence across diverse wilderness skills and environments.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate expert-level wilderness skills; navigate complex terrain confidently; respond effectively to wilderness emergencies; adapt skills across varied environments and seasons.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate expert-level proficiency across core wilderness skills — navigation, campcraft, water crossing, risk assessment, and emergency response.
I can navigate confidently through complex terrain using maps, compass, and landscape interpretation — including night navigation and poor-visibility situations.
I can establish and maintain safe camps in challenging conditions, including winter camping, alpine environments, and remote locations.
I can respond effectively to wilderness emergencies using wilderness first aid, appropriate equipment, and sound judgment.
I can adapt my skills across seasons, environments, and expedition types — from summer backpacking to winter mountaineering to water-based expeditions.

categories.name: Environmental Science and Ecological Knowledge
categories.raw: Understanding BC ecosystems, environmental processes, and applying ecological knowledge in outdoor contexts.

competencies.raw: Understand BC ecosystems and ecological processes; apply First Peoples ecological knowledge; recognize environmental impacts; contribute to conservation and environmental stewardship.
i_can_statements: I can identify and describe BC's major ecosystems — coastal temperate rainforests, interior forests, alpine zones, and wetlands — and understand their characteristics.
I can explain ecological processes including nutrient cycling, succession, predator-prey relationships, and human impacts on ecosystems.
I can recognize and interpret First Peoples ecological knowledge about land stewardship, sustainable resource use, and reciprocal relationships with nature.
I can assess human impacts on wilderness areas — from recreation impacts to climate change — and discuss conservation strategies.
I can contribute to conservation work — habitat restoration, invasive species management, or environmental monitoring — during outdoor experiences.

categories.name: Outdoor Education and Community Contribution
categories.raw: Using outdoor education and leadership to contribute to community learning and environmental awareness.

competencies.raw: Design and lead outdoor education programs; understand outdoor therapy and transformative learning; advocate for outdoor access and environmental education; explore outdoor careers.
i_can_statements: I can design and lead outdoor education programs that support learning outcomes — environmental knowledge, skills development, or personal growth.
I can understand and apply principles of transformative outdoor learning — using wilderness challenge to catalyze personal and social growth.
I can advocate for outdoor education access and equitable opportunities for diverse community members to engage with nature.
I can explore career pathways in outdoor education, environmental science, wilderness therapy, recreation management, and environmental conservation.
I can articulate how outdoor experiences have shaped my values, worldview, and sense of purpose, and how I might contribute to outdoor leadership.

areas.name: Expedition Planning and Logistics
areas.raw: Comprehensive planning and management of multi-day wilderness expeditions.
topics: Route planning and analysis: assessing terrain difficulty, elevation gain, water crossings, and logistical challenges
Supply management: calculating food and fuel needs, optimizing pack weight, and managing supply logistics
Group selection and preparation: assessing participant skills, providing pre-trip training, and setting clear expectations
Contingency planning: identifying potential hazards, developing response plans, and preparing for emergencies
Expedition timeline and pacing: balancing daily distances, rest days, and activity sequencing for participant development
Permits and regulations: understanding wilderness area regulations, permits, and responsible access protocols

areas.name: Advanced Technical Skills and Specializations
areas.raw: Developing expert-level competence in specialized wilderness skills.
topics: Winter wilderness skills: winter camping, avalanche awareness, winter travel techniques, and seasonal hazards
Alpine and mountain skills: elevation gain management, rock scrambling, exposure management, and mountain hazards
Water-based expeditions: paddling skills, water crossing techniques, and water safety systems
Navigation in complex environments: advanced map interpretation, GPS use, and navigation in featureless terrain
Emergency and rescue skills: wilderness first responder certification, rescue techniques, and emergency communication
Specialized equipment: understanding and using specialized gear for different expedition types and environments

areas.name: BC Ecosystems and Environmental Science
areas.raw: Understanding BC's diverse ecosystems, ecological processes, and environmental change.
topics: Biogeoclimatic zones: understanding BC's ecological classification system and major ecosystem types
Coastal temperate rainforest: understanding structure, species, and ecological processes of BC's unique rainforest
Interior forests: understanding dry interior and wet interior forest ecosystems, their characteristics, and ecological relationships
Alpine and subalpine environments: understanding high-elevation ecosystems, adaptation, and fragility
Aquatic ecosystems: understanding rivers, lakes, wetlands, and their ecological importance
Climate change and environmental monitoring: understanding climate impacts on BC ecosystems and contributing to ecological monitoring

areas.name: First Peoples Ecological Knowledge and Land Relationships
areas.raw: Understanding and honoring Indigenous perspectives on land stewardship and environmental relationships.
topics: Traditional ecological knowledge: understanding Indigenous management practices, resource use, and sustainability models
Seasonal harvesting and resource management: learning about traditional harvesting of plants, animals, and fish
Land-based cultural practices: understanding how movement, ceremonies, and cultural practices connect people to land
Colonization impacts on ecosystems: understanding how colonization disrupted Indigenous land management and ecological relationships
Reconciliation and co-management: understanding current efforts to restore Indigenous land management and decision-making
Supporting First Peoples communities: understanding how outdoor recreation can support or conflict with Indigenous rights and interests

areas.name: Wilderness Leadership and Group Development
areas.raw: Managing groups effectively and facilitating learning and development through outdoor experiences.
topics: Group dynamics and team development: understanding stages of group development and strategies for building cohesion
Outdoor teaching and mentoring: facilitating skill development, knowledge construction, and personal growth
Challenge, support, and resilience: balancing appropriate challenge with adequate support for growth
Conflict resolution and peer support: addressing conflicts, supporting peers in difficulty, and fostering inclusive community
Diversity and inclusion: creating expeditions and programs that welcome and serve people of diverse backgrounds and abilities
Transformative learning: understanding how outdoor experiences catalyze perspective shifts and personal development

areas.name: Outdoor Careers and Environmental Stewardship
areas.raw: Exploring careers in outdoor education and contributing to environmental conservation.
topics: Outdoor education and interpretation: careers teaching environmental knowledge and outdoor skills
Environmental conservation careers: work in parks management, habitat restoration, and environmental monitoring
Wilderness therapy and adventure therapy: using outdoor experiences therapeutically for mental health and social development
Recreation management: careers managing outdoor facilities, programs, and services
Environmental science and research: careers studying ecosystems, wildlife, and environmental change
Advocacy and policy work: careers advancing environmental protection, Indigenous rights, and equitable outdoor access
